fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and strong analyst estimates are offset by significant operating losses and a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY and analyst estimates of 109.4% for current quarter and 441.7% for current fiscal year indicate strong top-line expansion.
PEG ratio of 0.78 suggests growth is not excessively priced in relative to forward earnings estimates.
Strong liquidity with current ratio of 3.83 and quick ratio of 2.85, supported by $439.70M cash and manageable debt.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $151.30, though the high target is $220.00.
negatives: Operating margin of -8.6% and profit margin of -8.5% indicate the company is not yet profitable.
Return on equity of -6.1% and return on assets of -3.3% show poor returns on capital.
Free cash flow is negative at $-445.58M TTM, suggesting cash burn.
Forward P/E of 36.55 and Price/Sales of 27.61 are high, indicating aggressive valuation.
Last quarter's EPS missed estimates by 44.8%.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen a significant surge, up 635.5% in six months, with recent headlines highlighting its AI lease revenues and Mark Cuban's focus on foundational AI. Despite this strong upward momentum, some AI leaders, including AAOI, experienced a sell-off on May 19th. The company is approaching its next earnings date on August 6th, which could be a catalyst for further price action.
